_comment: Cloudflare WAF rules for ChiefLitigator API (chieflitigator.com / chieflitigator.ai). Deploy via Cloudflare API or dashboard. All rules are defense-in-depth layers protecting legal data endpoints.

firewall_rules.description: Block SQL injection attempts in query parameters and request body. ChiefLitigator APIs accept structured JSON only -- SQL syntax in input is always malicious.
firewall_rules.expression: (http.request.uri.query contains "UNION SELECT") or (http.request.uri.query contains "OR 1=1") or (http.request.uri.query contains "DROP TABLE") or (http.request.uri.query contains "--") or (http.request.uri.query contains ";") or (http.request.uri.query contains "xp_cmdshell") or (lower(http.request.uri.query) contains "union%20select") or (lower(http.request.uri.query) contains "or%201%3D1")

firewall_rules.description: Block XSS attempts. Legal document content should never contain script tags or event handlers in API requests.
firewall_rules.expression: (http.request.uri.query contains "<script") or (http.request.uri.query contains "javascript:") or (http.request.uri.query contains "onerror=") or (http.request.uri.query contains "onload=") or (http.request.uri.query contains "eval(") or (lower(http.request.uri.query) contains "%3Cscript")

firewall_rules.description: Block path traversal attempts. Prevents access to files outside the application root (e.g., /etc/passwd, ../ sequences).
firewall_rules.expression: (http.request.uri.path contains "..") or (http.request.uri.path contains "/etc/") or (http.request.uri.path contains "/proc/") or (http.request.uri.path contains "/.env") or (http.request.uri.path contains "/.git")

firewall_rules.description: Block common vulnerability scanners and exploit tools by User-Agent. These tools never make legitimate requests to a legal API.
firewall_rules.expression: (lower(http.request.headers["user-agent"]) contains "sqlmap") or (lower(http.request.headers["user-agent"]) contains "nikto") or (lower(http.request.headers["user-agent"]) contains "nessus") or (lower(http.request.headers["user-agent"]) contains "masscan") or (lower(http.request.headers["user-agent"]) contains "zgrab") or (lower(http.request.headers["user-agent"]) contains "dirbuster")

firewall_rules.description: Rate limit API endpoints to 100 requests per minute per IP. Prevents brute force, credential stuffing, and API abuse. Health check endpoints are excluded.
firewall_rules.expression: (http.request.uri.path matches "^/api/.*") and not (http.request.uri.path eq "/api/health") and not (http.request.uri.path eq "/api/health/ready")

firewall_rules.description: Strict rate limit on authentication endpoints. 10 requests per minute per IP prevents credential stuffing and brute force attacks.
Challenge suspicious traffic patterns. Requests without standard headers or with anomalous characteristics get a JS challenge before proceeding.
firewall_rules.expression: (not http.request.headers["accept"] contains "application/json" and http.request.uri.path matches "^/api/.*" and not http.request.uri.path eq "/api/health") or (http.request.headers["user-agent"] eq "") or (not any(http.request.headers["accept-language"][*] ne ""))

firewall_rules.description: Allow-list health check endpoints. These must be accessible for load balancer health probes and uptime monitoring without WAF interference.
Geographic restriction: US-only access. Legal data is subject to US jurisdiction. Enable this rule for production deployments with US-only client base. Disable if serving international clients.
firewall_rules.expression: (not ip.geoip.country in {"US" "PR" "GU" "VI" "AS" "MP"}) and (http.request.uri.path matches "^/api/.*") and not (http.request.uri.path eq "/api/health")
firewall_rules._note: Disabled by default. Enable for US-only deployments. Includes US territories (PR, GU, VI, AS, MP).

firewall_rules.description: Block requests with excessively large payloads. Legal documents have a practical size limit. Payloads over 50MB are likely attacks or misconfigured clients.

managed_rulesets._comment: Cloudflare managed rulesets provide continuously updated protection against known vulnerabilities.

owasp_core_ruleset._comment: OWASP Core Rule Set catches broad classes of web attacks. High sensitivity appropriate for legal data.

cloudflare_managed_ruleset._comment: Cloudflare's own threat intelligence rules, updated automatically.

cloudflare_exposed_credentials_check._comment: Checks login requests against known credential breaches.

page_rules.description: Force HTTPS and set security headers for all API traffic.
